commands.review_summary:
The branch captures restricted cron creator tool surfaces, persists caps on `agentTurn` add/update payloads across agent, gateway, skill, MCP, and embedded runtime paths, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +276, Tests +504. Total +780 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, but not from a live run in this review. Current main creates and updates cron `agentTurn` payloads without stamping the creator cap, while isolated cron execution later honors only persisted `payload.toolsAllow`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review should require contributor real-behavior proof and decide the compatibility impact; there is no narrow automation repair after the latest head.

target.title: fix(cron): preserve scheduled turn tool policy [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve the effective creator tool surface when agent-created cron `agentTurn` jobs are persisted, so delayed scheduled turns keep the same tool boundary as the creator.
- Apply the same cap to cron payload edits and to the standalone OpenClaw tools MCP cron surface.
- Add focused regression coverage for cron payload capping and policy-pipeline wiring.
- AI-assisted.

## Linked context

No public issue linked.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: scheduled agent turns created from a narrower tool surface retain that surface for the later run.
- Real environment tested: local source checkout with focused Vitest coverage.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: 2 test files passed, 167 tests passed.
- Observed result after fix: cron add/update payloads are capped to the creator tool surface, and policy-pipeline tests verify the cap is populated from restricted group/deny policy surfaces.
- What was not tested: full live channel/Gateway run and GitHub CI are still pending.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: local proof uses focused unit coverage for the changed boundary.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts src/agents/agent-tools.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/mcp/openclaw-tools-serve.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes. Scheduled agent turns created from a narrower tool surface now persist that same runtime tool cap.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did tool execution behavior change? Yes.
- Highest-risk area: existing cron `agentTurn` creation flows that intentionally rely on a narrower creator tool surface.
- How is that risk mitigated? Unrestricted creators are unchanged; the new cap is only populated when the source tool policy has a restrictive allowlist, denylist, or runtime allowlist, and focused tests cover add, update, allow-policy, and deny-policy cases.

## Current review state

- Next action: wait for automated checks and maintainer review.
- Still waiting on: GitHub CI and broader validation gates.
- Bot or reviewer comments addressed: none yet.
